bed: noun. A piece of furniture, usually flat and soft, on which to rest or sleep.
bedded: adjective. Furnished with one or more beds; Having (a particular number or type of) beds
bee: noun. A flying insect, of the clade Anthophila within the hymenopteran superfamily Apoidea, known for its organised societies (though only
deb: noun. A debutante (a young woman who makes her first formal appearance in society).
deed: noun. An action or act; something that is done.
deem: verb. To hold in belief or estimation; to adjudge as a conclusion; to regard as being; to evaluate according to one's beliefs; to account.
deemed: verb. simple past and past participle of deem
deme: noun. A township or other subdivision of ancient Attica.
embed: verb. To lay (something) as in a bed; to lay in surrounding matter; to bed.
embedded: adjective. Part of; firmly, or securely surrounded; lodged solidly into; deep-rooted.
meed: noun. A payment or recompense made for services rendered or in recognition of some achievement; reward; award.
bde: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Bade.
beme: noun. A mediaeval trumpet, usually used in hunting or warfare.
debe: noun. A tin container used for kerosene etc.
dmd: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Madhi Madhi.
mbd: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Dibabawon Manobo.
